paper_title: A WORKFLOW FOR LARGE-SCALE PERMANENT SLIDE IMAGING AND IMAGE ANALYSIS FOR DIATOM MORPHOMETRICS

methods.description: A semi-automated workflow for light-microscopic imaging, measurement, and analysis of diatom valves using a two-cycle slide scanning and image analysis process.

steps.description: Preparation of permanent slides by embedding oxidized frustule samples into a mounting medium.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare permanent slides with oxidized frustule samples

steps.description: Perform a low-resolution area scan to locate valves of interest using a 20× magnification objective.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct low-resolution area scan to locate diatom valves

steps.description: Create a focus map by determining optimal focus positions over a 2 mm grid.
steps.searchable_summary: create focus map for optimal imaging

steps.description: Perform fine focusing and capture images at multiple focus positions, stacking them to create extended focus depth images.
steps.searchable_summary: capture and stack images for extended focus depth

steps.description: Use SHERPA software to locate diatom valves in the stacked images.
steps.searchable_summary: locate diatom valves using SHERPA software

steps.description: Stitch images to create a virtual slide.
steps.searchable_summary: stitch images to form a virtual slide

steps.description: Export coordinates and morphometric feature descriptors of selected objects using SHERPA.
Process CSV output from SHERPA using R scripts for morphometric analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze morphometric data using R scripts
